feature_name: Dual operation mode: Root Complex and End Point
description: Supports operation as a PCIe Root Complex (RC) or End Point (EP) selectable via bootstrap pins and software override. As an EP it may run as legacy or native PCIe endpoint with behavior controlled by configuration space. Software can switch modes after boot using device control bits prior to link training. Mode choice sets header type (Type 0 for EP, Type 1 for RC) and governs permissible transactions. This flexibility enables the same silicon to serve host or device roles in embedded PCIe systems.
dependencies: PCIESSMODE bootstrap; device DEVSTAT (device-specific); CMD_STATUS.LTSSM_EN
Remarks: Select RC for root roles and EP for device roles, and follow the corresponding initialization sequence strictly.

feature_name: Link width and speed (x1/x2, Gen1/Gen2 2.5/5.0 GT/s)
description: Implements a single bidirectional link up to ×2 lanes with 2.5 or 5.0 GT/s per lane. Dynamic width conversion and lane reversal are supported to aid routing and interoperability. Gen2 operation is entered by directing a speed change after Gen1 link-up via PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD. Negotiated speed and width are readable in LINK_STAT_CTRL to verify link state. Ultra-low transmit and receive latency are stated as design goals for efficient data movement.
dependencies: Stable REFCLK; SerDes PLL lock; link training; COMMON_CLK_CFG as needed
Remarks: Set lane mode and speed policy before enabling LTSSM, and validate negotiated LINK_SPEED and width in status.

feature_name: Max payload and read request sizes
description: Outbound maximum payload is 128 bytes while inbound maximum payload is 256 bytes. Maximum remote read request size is 256 bytes and affects completer splitting and throughput. Requester Max Payload and Max Read Request sizes are programmed in DEV_STAT_CTRL during enumeration. Software and DMA must segment transfers to respect these limits. Planning for these sizes avoids back-pressure and improves sustained bandwidth.
dependencies: Enumeration software sets MAX_PAYLD and MAX_REQ_SZ; peer capability exchange
Remarks: Size EDMA and CPU bursts to fit 128-byte outbound chunks and align to peer-compliant limits.

feature_name: Endian conversion for inbound/outbound
description: PCIe core is little-endian at the pins while the subsystem supports big-endian and little-endian systems. Programmable swizzle on 1/2/4/8-byte boundaries is provided for inbound and outbound paths. The ENDIAN register selects the swap granularity when operating in big-endian environments. Correct configuration preserves data ordering across the PCIe boundary. Validation with known patterns is recommended before enabling high-rate traffic.
dependencies: System endianness choice; program ENDIAN before transfers
Remarks: Pick the minimum required swap granularity and confirm by loopback or memory test patterns.

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions)
description: Outbound hardware maps internal bus addresses to PCIe memory through 32 equal-sized regions. OB_SIZE selects 1/2/4/8 MB region granularity and selects index bits used from the local address. Each region uses OB_OFFSET_INDEXn and OB_OFFSETn_HI to create 32- or 64-bit PCIe base addresses. Transactions must not straddle region boundaries because mapping is computed only at issuance. Software should configure all required regions before enabling OB_XLT_EN.
dependencies: OB_SIZE and OB_OFFSET* programmed; CMD_STATUS.OB_XLT_EN=1
Remarks: Plan contiguous windows to minimize regions and prevent boundary crossing in large DMA bursts.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions, BAR association)
description: Inbound mapping translates accepted PCIe addresses to internal addresses using four regions. Regions are selected by BAR matches via IB_BARn and use IB_STARTn_{HI,LO} plus IB_OFFSETn to compute local targets. BAR0 is an exception that is always mapped to application registers and cannot be remapped. For 64-bit windows, adjacent BARs are paired (e.g., BAR2+BAR3) to hold the full address. Configure regions and enable IB_XLT_EN before allowing inbound bus mastering.
dependencies: BARs programmed; IB_* registers set; CMD_STATUS.IB_XLT_EN=1
Remarks: Use BAR1+ for data windows and keep start addresses ordered if mapping non-contiguous ranges.

feature_name: Configurable BAR mask and BAR behavior override (DBI_CS2)
description: BAR mask registers let software predefine BAR size and type prior to RC enumeration. Writes are enabled by setting CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2, then programming BARx_MASK and the corresponding BARx. BAR0 mask is limited because BAR0 is dedicated to application registers in both modes. Readback of BARs (not the masks) is used to verify effect by test patterns. Clear DBI_CS2 after configuration to restore normal DBI behavior.
dependencies: CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2=1 during programming; proper ordering; verification readback
Remarks: Document requested BAR apertures and ensure RC policies align with device mask settings.

feature_name: Configuration and IO access windows (Address Space 0)
description: A 16 KB Address Space 0 contains 4 KB application registers, 4 KB local configuration, 4 KB remote configuration, and a 4 KB RC-only I/O window. Remote windows require a live link and only support single 32-bit accesses (no bursts). The IOBASE register provides the base for outbound I/O TLPs in RC mode. Software should treat these windows as non-cacheable to avoid reordering issues. Accesses must be gated until link training is complete to prevent stalls.
dependencies: LIVE link; CFG_SETUP set for targets; IOBASE programmed (RC)
Remarks: Wrap these spaces with guards so code does not touch them when link is down or unenumerated.

feature_name: Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI) generation and reception
description: Up to 32 MSI vectors are supported for both generation and reception in RC and EP modes. MSI address and data come from MSI capability registers and must be configured by system software. EP generates MSI by writing the vector to RC’s MSI_IRQ aperture mapped in BAR0. RC/EP status bits are in MSIn_IRQ_STATUS with write-one-to-clear semantics and require IRQ_EOI writes. Disable INTx when MSI_EN is set to prevent duplicate signaling.
dependencies: MSI_CAP.MSI_EN=1; MSI address/data set; MSIn enable/status; IRQ_EOI
Remarks: Ensure posted write ordering does not race MSI generation by using reads or fences as appropriate.

feature_name: Legacy INTx interrupt support (RC receive, EP generate)
description: RC receives INTA/B/C/D messages from downstream devices and must poll configuration to identify the source. EP can assert and deassert a selected INTx using EP_IRQ_SET and EP_IRQ_CLR. Only one pending legacy interrupt per function is allowed until deassert is observed. The LEGACY_X IRQ block provides raw, enabled, and enable set/clear registers. EOI ordering must account for potential re-trigger if deassert has not yet propagated.
dependencies: INT_PIN configured; LEGACY_X enables set; IRQ_EOI handling
Remarks: Probe device cause registers before EOI to avoid spurious retriggering on slow paths.

feature_name: Error, power/reset, and link event interrupts (14 total lines)
description: The block exposes 14 interrupt event lines including MSI groups, legacy groups, error groups, and PM/reset events. Error events include system, fatal, non-fatal, correctable, advanced error, and AXI bridge conditions. Power and link events include PME turnoff/ack, PMEs, and link reset requests. Status and enable/clear are provided via ERR_IRQ_* and PMRST_* registers with write-one-to-clear semantics. IRQ_EOI must be written with the event number to complete servicing.
dependencies: ERR/PMRST enable bits set; W1C status clears; EOI written
Remarks: Map high-priority events to fast CPU vectors and log context before clearing status.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) and logging
description: Baseline and Advanced Error Reporting provide fine-grained status, masking, and severity classification. Uncorrectable and correctable error status/mask registers capture conditions and allow filtering. ECRC enable controls and First Error Pointer assist with diagnosis alongside four DW header logs. RC collects error category counts and captures the reporting Requester ID for root-cause triage. Software can choose SERR or PME-based signaling via ROOT_CTRL_CAP and ROOT_ERR_CMD.
dependencies: Enable *_RPT and *_EN bits; PCIE_UNCERR/CERR *_MASK/SVRTY; PCIE_ACCR
Remarks: Dump HDR_LOGx on error and clear W1C flags in a defined order to avoid losing first error context.

feature_name: ECRC generation and checking
description: End-to-End CRC generation and checking are supported when both endpoints agree. Controls are PCIE_ACCR.ECRC_GEN_EN and PCIE_ACCR.ECRC_CHK_EN with capability bits indicating support. Mismatched configuration can cause false error reporting and interrupt storms. Disable ECRC when peers lack capability, especially in RC mode managing heterogeneous EPs. Enable only after initial bring-up to simplify early diagnostics.
dependencies: Peer capability matching; consistent enable across link
Remarks: Coordinate ECRC policy during enumeration and record it in system inventory for supportability.

feature_name: Power management: device and link (ASPM L0s/L1, D-states)
description: Device supports D0, optional D1/D2, and D3hot/D3cold states with PME signaling. Link supports ASPM L0s and L1 plus software-driven transitions with common clock considerations. PMCAP and PM_CTL_STAT program device capabilities and current power state. ACTIVE_LINK_PM and COMMON_CLK_CFG in LINK_STAT_CTRL govern link power behavior. L2 state is not supported and should not be requested by software.
dependencies: Platform power policy; common clock if SSC; PM and Link control fields
Remarks: Balance residency targets against exit latency and ensure drivers quiesce traffic before D3.

feature_name: DMA via VBUSM master/slave (no internal DMA)
description: The subsystem exposes a 128-bit slave for outbound requests and a 128-bit master for inbound completions. External EDMA or CPU engines drive high-throughput transfers over these ports. Throughput of about 85% of 2.0/4.0 Gbps per lane is achievable considering 8b/10b and protocol overhead. EDMA examples illustrate memory write and read configuration with 128-byte payloads. Ensure bus mastering is enabled prior to issuing any outbound read or write TLPs.
dependencies: Configured address translation; EDMA setup; STATUS_COMMAND.BUS_MS=1
Remarks: Segment large moves into 128-byte chunks and align to 8-byte boundaries to prevent splits.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback modes
description: PIPE loopback allows master/slave symbol-level tests between two devices or a tester. PHY loopback routes transmitter data back to the receiver internally and can run without a link partner. PL_LINK_CTRL.LPBK_EN controls PIPE loopback, and SerDes lane registers configure PHY loopback. PL_FORCE_LINK can force LTSSM states to enable compliance testing sequences. Configure loopback before any TLP traffic to avoid sequence number mismatches and drops.
dependencies: Disable user traffic; set LPBK_EN or SerDes loopback; force link state as needed
Remarks: Avoid sending configuration or completion traffic during loopback to prevent spurious errors.

feature_name: Reset and hot reset control
description: Both hardware and software resets are supported with sticky bit behavior noted. RSTCMD.INIT_RST issues a downstream hot reset and FLUSH_N indicates pending transactions on the bridge. After any reset, software must wait at least 100 ms and up to 1.5 s in worst cases before retrying traffic. Following reset, link training and configuration must be repeated as appropriate. Observing these delays prevents false timeouts and mis-enumeration.
dependencies: Quiesced traffic; FLUSH_N=0; reinit and retrain after reset
Remarks: Sequence resets from RC where possible and coordinate with peers to avoid spurious UR/CPL ABORTs.

feature_name: Clocking and SerDes PLL configuration/status
description: The PHY requires a clean differential REFCLK into the SerDes PLL (typically 100 MHz with SSC options). PLL control uses PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL with ENPLL to start and MPY to set multiplication for target line rate. Lock indication is provided by PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK and lane-level status bits. Common clock usage must be reflected in LINK_STAT_CTRL.COMMON_CLK_CFG for correct training behavior. Only start LTSSM after stable PLL lock and verified reference clock quality.
dependencies: Stable REFCLK; PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL and STS used; COMMON_CLK_CFG set
Remarks: Follow reference clock jitter guidance and defer training until LOCK is asserted to all lanes.

feature_name: Transaction and burst alignment rules
description: Outbound transactions must be 8-byte aligned and no larger than 128 bytes, with misaligned writes capped at 120 bytes. Only incremental burst types are supported on the internal bus; fixed and wrap bursts are undefined. The requester should size reads at or below the Max Read Request to avoid interleaving. Byte enables must be a single contiguous string of ones for any write. Nonconforming bursts can lock the bus or corrupt data and must be filtered at the source.
dependencies: DMA and CPU initiators configured for incremental aligned bursts
Remarks: Validate burst templates during integration tests and enforce with driver assertions.

feature_name: Master privilege and priority control
description: Inbound master transactions can be marked supervisor-only and carry a master PRIVID for policing. PRIORITY register sets MST_PRIV, MST_PRIVID, and inbound master priority with 0 highest and 7 lowest. This controls which transactions may access device registers versus memory regions. Combined with interconnect QoS, latency-sensitive traffic can be elevated over background flows. Proper use prevents unintended user-mode accesses to control planes.
dependencies: System privilege model and SoC interconnect QoS settings
Remarks: Set supervisor-only access for application registers and assign higher priority to real-time flows.

feature_name: Application and debug register access (BAR0 mapping)
description: Application registers are accessible locally and via BAR0 from both RC and EP roles. RC software can also access EP application registers through the upper 1 KB of EP configuration space. DEBUG0 and DEBUG1 provide LTSSM state, training indicators, and TX/RX diagnostics. Appendix A enumerates LTSSM state codes that map to DEBUG0.LTSSM_STATE. Protect the BAR0 window from unintended remote writes during production use.
dependencies: BAR0 mapped; link up for remote access; DEBUG regs readable
Remarks: Gate BAR0 exposure behind RC policy and disable writes where not needed for field diagnostics.

feature_name: Performance tuning: ACK/NFTS/timers and SKP
description: Programmable ACK frequency and the number of fast training sequences allow tailoring to common-clock links. Replay and latency timers can be increased via PL_ACKTIMER and SYM_NUM to improve robustness. Excessive tightening risks noncompliance and link instability with long traces or SSC. LINK_STAT_CTRL.COMMON_CLK_CFG and ACK_FREQ fields should reflect the actual clocking scheme. Changes should be validated with partner devices and compliance suites before deployment.
dependencies: Stable link; common clock use; spec timing limits respected
Remarks: Record tuned values per platform SKU and retest after any PHY or layout change.

feature_name: Message transmit and TLP filtering controls
description: PL_OMSG and PL_LINK_CTRL.OMSG_REQ permit software injection of PCIe Message TLPs for control and test. SYMTIMER_FLTMASK provides fine-grained filters for completion attribute checks, UR handling, CFG/IO/MSG acceptance, and watchdog behavior. FLT_MASK2 adds vendor message and DLLP abort handling controls. These features help debugging but can affect strict compliance if misused. Production settings should keep defaults unless specific interoperability issues are diagnosed.
dependencies: Default filter masks; trained link; message formats per spec
Remarks: Document any non-default filters and revisit after firmware or topology changes.

feature_name: Completion timeout support (ranges and disable)
description: DEV_CAP2 advertises supported completion timeout ranges and optional disable capability. DEV_STAT_CTRL2 programs the active timeout window and whether timeouts are disabled. The PCIe specification recommends not expiring below 10 ms to avoid false timeouts. Choose conservative defaults unless path analysis justifies tighter bounds. Always recheck settings when system latency or topology changes.
dependencies: Requester role; platform timeout policy
Remarks: Start with default 50 ms–50 s and only narrow after end-to-end validation across all devices.

feature_name: Power domain and module state transitions
description: Power-cycling the PCIe module resets internal state and requires full reinitialization on resume. All inbound and outbound traffic must be halted on both link partners before shutoff. After power-on, reconfigure SerDes and restart link training before allowing software accesses. Improper sequencing can hang the system and might require a device POR to recover. PSC status (GOSTAT) must be polled to confirm transitions before proceeding.
dependencies: PSC sequencing; quiesced link; PLL reinit and LTSSM restart
Remarks: Capture and restore software-visible configuration as needed to minimize resume time.
